2603.24936.title: TIGFlow-GRPO: Trajectory Forecasting via Interaction-Aware Flow Matching and Reward-Driven Optimization
2603.24936.paper_summary: Human trajectory forecasting is important for intelligent multimedia systems operating in visually complex environments, such as autonomous driving and crowd surveillance. Although Conditional Flow Matching (CFM) has shown strong ability in modeling trajectory distributions from spatio-temporal observations, existing approaches still focus primarily on supervised fitting, which may leave social norms and scene constraints insufficiently reflected in generated trajectories. To address this issue, we propose TIGFlow-GRPO, a two-stage generative framework that aligns flow-based trajectory generation with behavioral rules. In the first stage, we build a CFM-based predictor with a Trajectory-Interaction-Graph (TIG) module to model fine-grained visual-spatial interactions and strengthen context encoding. This stage captures both agent-agent and agent-scene relations more effectively, providing more informative conditional features for subsequent alignment. In the second stage, we perform Flow-GRPO post-training,where deterministic flow rollout is reformulated as stochastic ODE-to-SDE sampling to enable trajectory exploration, and a composite reward combines view-aware social compliance with map-aware physical feasibility. By evaluating trajectories explored through SDE rollout, GRPO progressively steers multimodal predictions toward behaviorally plausible futures. Experiments on the ETH/UCY and SDD datasets show that TIGFlow-GRPO improves forecasting accuracy and long-horizon stability while generating trajectories that are more socially compliant and physically feasible. These results suggest that the proposed framework provides an effective way to connect flow-based trajectory modeling with behavior-aware alignment in dynamic multimedia environments.
2603.24936.paper_summary_zh: 人類軌跡預測對於在視覺複雜環境中運作的智能多媒體系統非常重要，例如自動駕駛和人群監控。儘管條件流匹配（CFM）在從時空觀察中建模軌跡分佈方面顯示出強大的能力，但現有的方法仍主要集中在監督擬合上，這可能使社會規範和場景約束在生成的軌跡中反映不足。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了TIGFlow-GRPO，一個將基於流的軌跡生成與行為規則對齊的兩階段生成框架。在第一階段，我們建立了一個基於CFM的預測器，並結合了軌跡互動圖（TIG）模組，以建模細粒度的視覺空間互動並加強上下文編碼。這一階段更有效地捕捉了代理-代理和代理-場景之間的關係，為後續的對齊提供了更具信息性的條件特徵。在第二階段，我們進行流-GRPO後訓練，其中確定性流展開被重新表述為隨機ODE到SDE的採樣，以便實現軌跡探索，並且一個綜合獎勵將視圖感知的社會合規性與地圖感知的物理可行性結合起來。通過評估通過SDE展開探索的軌跡，GRPO逐步引導多模態預測朝向行為上合理的未來。在ETH/UCY和SDD數據集上的實驗表明，TIGFlow-GRPO提高了預測準確性和長期穩定性，同時生成了更符合社會規範和物理可行性的軌跡。這些結果表明，所提出的框架提供了一種有效的方法，將基於流的軌跡建模與動態多媒體環境中的行為感知對齊相連接。
2603.24936.author: Xuepeng Jing et.al.
2603.24936.authors: Xuepeng Jing, Wenhuan Lu, Hao Meng, Zhizhi Yu, Jianguo Wei
